title: hibbertia fasciculata var. prostrata
intro: Hibbertia fasciculata var. prostrata, also known as the creeping Guinea flower or dwarf Guinea flower, is a low-growing, prostrate shrub native to Australia. It is used in traditional medicine, particularly in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its purported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Hibbertia fasciculata var. prostrata are thought to include reduced inflammation and improved immune function, as well as potential antiseptic and antifungal effects. In herbal medicine, Hibbertia fasciculata var. prostrata can be prepared in various forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
